Criteria: A thunderstorm is producing or capable of producing hail 1 inch (quarter-sized) or larger, and/or wind gusts of 58 mph (50 knots) or stronger. Immediate shelter is required.
Conditions are favorable for the development of severe thunderstorms in and close to the watch area. Monitor local conditions closely.
Follow-up bulletin issued by local NWS offices providing real-time tracking, specific damage reports, or localized impact adjustments for active warnings.
A thunderstorm is producing strong winds (40-57 mph) and/or small hail (less than 1 inch). Conditions are sub-severe but pose minor localized risks.
Short-fuse atmospheric notice highlighting localized, short-duration hazards like frequent lightning, heavy downpours, or gusty winds that do not meet warning criteria.
An active warning flag denoting highest-tier threats: baseball-sized hail (2.75+ inches) and/or hurricane-force wind gusts (80+ mph). Automatically triggers Wireless Emergency Alerts (WEA).
An active warning flag denoting mid-tier severe threats: golf-ball-sized hail (1.75+ inches) and/or wind gusts of 70-79 mph.
